notes: Fixes the real 3060 wedge + timezone + job cap. TIMEZONE: docker-compose was set to TZ=UTC, so every stored timestamp was 9h behind — now TZ=JST-9 (UTC+9, no tzdata needed). JOB CAP: the fleet cap only counted each node's GPU workers, so total was stuck at the sum of GPU counts and the new CPU workers could never claim a job — now it counts GPU+CPU per node. 3060 WEDGE: node 2.28 hardens temp-drive enumeration — the spill path (used when the temp drive is low on space) called psutil.disk_partitions(), which hangs forever on Windows when a dead network drive exists, wedging the worker before Step 1 with no timeout; now it uses GetDriveTypeW (fixed drives only, never touches the network). Server also requeues never-started (0% for 6+ min) jobs faster. NOTE: the 3060 wedges because its temp drive C:\Byte_temp is low on space (triggering the spill); pointing its temp at a roomier drive avoids it even before updating.
